name: Phoenix and Dragon
recipeInstructions:
1. Lay 2 slices of ham on one side of the butterflied chicken breast. Refold the breast, enclosing the ham.
2. Lay the breast on a sheet of caul fat and roll it up tightly. The caul is sticky, so it needs nothing to seal it.
3. Dust the surface with water chestnut powder, flour or cornstarch. Shake off the excess.
4. Heat oil in a wok to 350°F (moderate). Slide the chicken breasts into the oil, one by one. When they have browned (about 5 minutes), remove one from the oil and press it between your fingers. If it feels mushy, it needs more cooking. Remove and drain the chicken.
5. Slice the chicken, crosswise diagonally, into 1/2-inch pieces.
6. To make the vegetable sauce: Heat oil in a wok and flavor with garlic and ginger. Remove the garlic and ginger and add the water chestnuts, bamboo shoots, and scallions to the wok. Stir-fry for 30 seconds, then add soy sauce, wine or sherry, and chicken broth. When the liquid boils, stir in the dissolved cornstarch to thicken the sauce.
7. Spread the sauce on a platter as a bed for the sliced chicken breast.
